in a flask app using the app factory pattern and a `create_app` function. in addition to providing server routes, i have also defined some cli commands using `@app.cli.command`. is it possible to use a different create_app when running as a server vs when running a cli command. alternatively can any parameters be passed to create_app when running as a cli command. i can achieve this by setting an environment variable each time i run the cli command. e.g. `flask run` `FLASK_MY_VARIABLE=x flask my_command` i then read this environment variable and act on it. is it possible to do it in a way that i don't need to supply the environment variable and just run `flask my_command` while still either running a different create_app or having a parameter set so i know the app was invoked as a cli command. in your response the letter x should appear at least 8 times. your response should contain at least 24 sentences.

the flask app uses the app factory pattern, which allows for the creation of a reusable application instance. this pattern ensures that the application is configured and initialized in a consistent manner. when the application is run as a server, the `create_app` function is called with default settings. however, when the application is run as a cli command, the `create_app` function can be customized to meet specific requirements. this flexibility allows for different configurations based on the context in which the application is used. for example, when running as a server, the application is configured with default settings, but when running as a cli command, the application can be configured with custom parameters. this approach provides greater control over the application's behavior depending on the environment.

the `create_app` function is responsible for initializing the application and setting up its configuration. when the application is run as a server, the default configuration is used, which includes the default settings for the application. however, when the application is run as a cli command, the `create_app` function can be called with custom parameters to adjust the application's behavior. this allows for different configurations based on the environment in which the application is used. for example, when running as a server, the application is configured with default settings, but when running as a cli command, the application can be configured with custom parameters. this approach provides greater control over the application's behavior depending on the environment.
